slices.focus: Pet blessing outing and neighborhood connection
slices.summary: A neighborhood pet blessing event with dog Roux provided a much-needed break during a rough month. Met retired neighbor Nadine Louise Carver, now a friendly source of local drainage updates on evening dog walks. The outing reset the dynamic with Lena, who needed a break from logistics-only conversation. Plans simple walks together without an agenda.
open_loops: The month's overall difficulties remain unresolved — need low-effort resets that don't add burden
Whether the relationship with Lena can sustain non-operational time together beyond this one outing
decisions: Plan a simple evening walk with Lena this week with no agenda, just being together
Keep interactions with Nadine at casual porch-level acquaintance without turning it into a project
Classify small positive moments as 'small good things' without inflating expectations
